what_it_does: This file stores a minimal YAML-frontmatter metadata record for a channel memory attachment, declaring it as an images attachment that points to an image asset in the design-source channel.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added timestamp
attachment id uses att- prefix: att-3e8e9c19
target is an image:// URI pointing to design-source/recent-pitches/p-34
added timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone offset
no body content beyond the frontmatter
standouts: References a specific pitch image asset via the image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-34 URI
Serves as a lightweight bridge between channel-memory attachments and design-source image assets
conventions: image:// URI scheme for media targets
ISO 8601 timestamp with explicit UTC offset
concerns: Contains no embedded or described image content; the actual asset is external
No title, caption, or MIME type for the image
No checksum or size to verify the referenced asset
notes: A tiny metadata stub that links the design-source channel to a recent-pitches image asset identified as p-34.
questions: What is the actual image file or format for p-34?
Who added this attachment and why?
Is p-34 a pitch deck, a single slide, or a concept image?
How is the image:// URI resolved to a physical file?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is an attachment record and it records an attachment pointing to an image target.
apparent_themes: Separation of metadata from media assets
intention_signals: It appears intended to catalog an image attachment in the design-source channel so the image can be located and associated with channel memory without storing the binary data inline.
novelty: The use of an image:// URI target scheme to reference a channel-scoped image asset rather than a filesystem path.
contribution: Provides a stable attachment identifier and a resolvable pointer to an image asset in the design-source channel, supporting retrieval and association while keeping the attachment record small.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter channel-memory attachment record linking att-3e8e9c19 in the design-source channel to the image asset image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-34 with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To register an image attachment in the design-source channel so it can be referenced alongside recent pitch materials.
